title: Project Miner Temp Power Approved Import Mutation Admission
objective: Admit and implement the narrow Project Miner Temp Power import mutation route after the PM Lane 277 approval row, writing only governed project, workpackage, task, and apparatus rows with deterministic idempotency and downstream stop boundaries.
active_role: VS Code Codex Technical Repo Authority / PM Stakeholder Delegate / Import Mutation Executor

source_floor.meaning: PM Lane 277 first approval-row execution is complete; approval status is approved_for_import_packet with one current approval row and import_authority not_admitted.

stakeholder_authority.summary: Jason instructed Codex to move forward with authority for approval of all blocker items that allows work on the predetermined PM lane framework and path end-to-end without stopping for approval at each step as repo technical authority and project stakeholder.
stakeholder_authority.bounded_interpretation: This packet is the separately packeted project-import admission required after PM Lane 277. It opens only the approved Temp Power import mutation route and preserves downstream write stops.

preflight_requirements: approval-status readback reports approved_for_import_packet and exactly one current approval row
hosted candidate readback matches pm-import-candidate-miner-temp-power and expected counts/fingerprints before any hosted import call
project-import route is deployed before hosted import call
downstream accessible counts are captured before hosted import call
validation_steps: Focused pytest for project import persistence, approval persistence, and admission plan
Route 404 proof before deploy or route availability proof after deploy
not_allowed: No assignments, field authorization, lead or crew selection, schedule/status mutation, durable field records, production tracking, customer reporting, completion evidence, billing, payroll, invoice, accounting, or external finance-system writes.
No source workbook or PDF edit or writeback.
No Render, Vercel, Olares, DNS, auth, ingress, or new service change from this shell.
